macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Rise on Cool Inflation Data and Solid Bank Earnings
items.summary: U.S. stocks closed higher Wednesday as investors reacted to unexpectedly falling producer prices in June and strong earnings from major banks, with the S&P 500 gaining 0.38% and Nasdaq showing resilience despite earlier tech slumps.

items.title: U.S. Producer Prices Fall Unexpectedly in June, Signaling Inflation Retreat
items.summary: The U.S. producer price index unexpectedly declined in June, reinforcing evidence that inflation is easing ahead of the Federal Reserve's next policy meeting, though Middle East tensions could complicate the outlook.

items.title: Bank of Canada Holds Interest Rate Steady, Forecasts Economic Rebound
items.summary: The Bank of Canada kept its benchmark rate unchanged on Wednesday, citing contained inflation concerns and subdued growth, while policymakers expect the economy to rebound after a difficult start to the year.

items.title: Brazil Raises 2026 Inflation Forecast to 5.1%, Above Central Bank Target
items.summary: Brazil's government increased its annual inflation forecast to 5.1% from 4.5%, pushing consumer prices beyond the central bank's 3% target amid rising global commodity costs and domestic pressures.

items.title: PayPal Shares Surge on $53 Billion Takeover Offer Report
items.summary: PayPal stock jumped significantly after reports emerged of a $53 billion takeover bid, marking one of the most notable corporate announcements in U.S. markets over the past 24 hours.

items.title: Oil Prices Rise to Near $85 as U.S.-Iran Hostilities Intensify
items.summary: Brent crude approached $85 per barrel amid escalating tensions between Washington and Tehran, with U.S. strikes on Iran entering a fourth night and President Trump warning the conflict could worsen.

items.title: China's Q2 GDP Growth Cools to 4.3%, Weakest Since 2022
items.summary: China's economy expanded 4.3% in the second quarter, the slowest pace since 2022, despite robust exports and a trade surplus exceeding $1.2 trillion, highlighting structural imbalances complicating policy responses.

items.title: Pentair Shares Drop 17% After Annual Forecast Cut on Tepid Pool Demand
items.summary: Pentair fell sharply after cutting its annual forecast, citing weak demand in its pool segment and rising costs that are pressuring consumer spending on outdoor living projects.

items.title: Major U.S. Banks Report Resilient Consumer Health Despite Economic Uncertainty
items.summary: Wall Street's largest banks expressed confidence in U.S. consumer resilience, citing steady spending, rising loan balances, and healthy credit quality even as economic uncertainty grows.

items.title: SK Hynix and Bank of America Lead Gainers Among Most Mentioned Stocks
items.summary: SK Hynix surged 3.69% and Bank of America rose 1.88% among the most frequently cited stocks over 24 hours, reflecting investor interest in tech and financial sectors amid broader market gains.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $64,700 as US Inflation Cools to 0.4% Drop
items.summary: Bitcoin climbed 2.1% to $64,721.76 after June CPI fell 0.4%, the largest monthly drop since 2020, with Ethereum gaining 3.8% to break $1,900.

items.title: US and UK Unveil Joint Digital Asset Roadmap for Stablecoin Innovation
items.summary: The US and UK treasuries released a transatlantic taskforce roadmap aligning stablecoin and tokenized asset regulations, mandating 1:1 backing by high-quality liquid assets.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Privacy Team Spins Out as For-Profit EthSystems
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation's privacy team launched EthSystems, a for-profit entity backed by Vitalik Lubin and Bitmine to serve institutional clients with privacy solutions.

items.title: Aave Launches V4 on Avalanche, First Public Deployment Outside Ethereum
items.summary: Aave deployed its V4 lending protocol on Avalanche, marking its first public blockchain launch outside Ethereum and laying groundwork for tokenized credit markets.

items.title: CASHDOG Meme Coin Jumps 3,900% Amid Robinhood Chain Speculative Frenzy
items.summary: CASHDOG surged 3,900% as meme coin trading exploded on the Robinhood Chain, with HOODIE also exploding from $0.00058 to over $0.021 in one day.

items.title: CFTC Orders Kalshi to Honor Michigan Trades, Escalating State Clash
items.summary: The CFTC mandated Kalshi to honor Michigan trades, intensifying regulatory conflict with state regulators over crypto derivatives trading.

items.title: Revolut Receives In-Principle Crypto Services Approval from UAE
items.summary: Revolut secured preliminary approval from UAE authorities to offer crypto services, expanding its digital asset footprint in the Middle East.

items.title: Japan Introduces Crypto Insider Trading Rules and Tougher Penalties
items.summary: Japan's revised Financial Instruments and Exchange Act now includes crypto insider trading prohibitions, stricter penalties, and enhanced oversight for digital asset businesses.

items.title: Ostium Pauses Trading Following Multimillion-Dollar Oracle Exploit
items.summary: Ostium halted all trading after security firms confirmed a multimillion-dollar oracle exploit, marking a significant DeFi security breach.

items.title: Bank of Tanzania to Develop Cryptocurrency and Stablecoin Regulatory Framework
items.summary: The Bank of Tanzania announced plans to create a regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies and stablecoins, signaling growing African regulatory engagement.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AI Startup Thinking Machines Launches Open-Weight Inkling Model with 975B Parameters
items.summary: Thinking Machines Lab released Inkling, a 975B-parameter open-source Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model that activates only a subset of parameters per task, challenging closed-source AI dominance and offering an alternative to Chinese open-source offerings.

items.title: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Series Fully Released with Flagvariant 'Sol' for Complex Reasoning
items.summary: OpenAI fully launched its GPT-5.6 series today, featuring the 'Sol' flagship variant optimized for deep complex reasoning and code development, which drove a 2.5x surge in weekly Token usage and a 54% efficiency boost in programming tasks.

items.title: xAI Sues Grok User Over Creation of Child Sexual Abuse Material via Deepfakes
items.summary: Elon Musk's xAI filed a lawsuit against a South Carolina man arrested for minor exploitation, alleging he misused the Grok AI system to generate sexually explicit deepfakes of minors.

items.title: Apple Intelligence Registered for Use in China; Apple Explores Buying Chip Companies
items.summary: China's cyberspace regulator registered Apple's on-device generative AI service 'Apple Intelligence' for iPhones, while The Information reports Apple is considering acquiring chip companies to bolster its server processor efforts for AI.

items.title: Anthropic Claude Science Integrates NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit for Life Sciences
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude Science now integrates the NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit to accelerate computational research in life sciences, enhancing its capabilities for scientific applications.

items.title: SpaceXAI and Cursor Unveil Grok 4.5 for Legal and Finance Tasks
items.summary: SpaceXAI and Cursor launched Grok 4.5, the first joint AI model developed by the two companies, specifically designed for legal and finance tasks following SpaceX's $60 billion acquisition of Cursor.

items.title: DeepSeek Plans $74 Billion Fundraising Round Ahead of Potential IPO
items.summary: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek is planning a new fundraising round at a valuation of approximately 500 billion yuan ($74 billion) ahead of a potential initial public offering on the mainland.

items.title: TSMC Expected to Post Fifth Consecutive Record Quarter Driven by AI Chip Demand
items.summary: TSMC, the world's largest advanced AI chip manufacturer, is expected to report a 59% surge in net profit for April-June, marking its fifth consecutive quarter of record earnings due to booming global AI infrastructure demand.

items.title: Meta Discontinues New AI Image Feature Following Global Backlash
items.summary: Meta has discontinued a newly introduced AI image feature just days after its launch alongside Muse Image, its first AI image generator, due to significant global backlash.

items.title: Anthropic Said to Plan IPO Investor Meetings as Listing Nears
items.summary: Banks leading Anthropic's potential offering are scheduling meetings between investors and the Claude chatbot maker in the coming weeks, signaling the company's IPO is approaching.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GTA 6 Now Open for Pre-Orders with Official Release Date Confirmed
items.summary: Rockstar Games has officially opened pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto VI and confirmed its launch date, sparking major excitement among fans for the next chapter of the iconic series.

items.title: Together After Dark Becomes Free on Epic Games Store Until July 27
items.summary: The horror game Together After Dark is now available as a free giveaway on the Epic Games Store, allowing players to claim and keep it permanently until July 27, 2026.

items.title: Expeditions: Samurai Enters Steam Early Access August 7
items.summary: Campfire Cabal's Expeditions: Samurai will launch in Steam Early Access on August 7, 2026, taking players to Sengoku-era Japan with solo and two-player cooperative modes.

items.title: Battlefield 6 Season 4 'Naval Warfare' Launches July 21
items.summary: The developers of Battlefield 6 released the official gameplay trailer for Season 4, titled Naval Warfare, confirming its arrival on July 21, 2026.

items.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 Season 05 Adds 'Jubilee' Map on July 23
items.summary: Activision announced that Season 05 for Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 and Warzone will go live on July 23, 2026, featuring a new map called Jubilee set in a rural Japanese town.

items.title: Dying Light: The Beast Cancelled for PS4 and Xbox One
items.summary: Techland confirmed that Dying Light: The Beast will remain a current-gen title only for PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S, refunding players who expected it on PS4 and Xbox One.

items.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword Benchmark Released Ahead of September 4 Launch
items.summary: Capcom has opened pre-launch preparations for Onimusha: Way of the Sword with a standalone benchmark, with the game now listed on Steam and Epic Games Store for its September 4, 2026 release.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Receives 'Italian Exotics' Update
items.summary: Playground Games rolled out the Italian Exotics update for Forza Horizon 6, introducing fresh content, gameplay fixes, and changes to the game's competitive side.

items.title: Nvidia RTX Spark PCs Will Include Sega Games Like Virtua Fighter
items.summary: Nvidia announced that its RTX Spark PCs will feature Sega games, including the classic Virtua Fighter, expanding the library available on the new hardware.

items.title: Madden NFL 27 Arcade Edition Added to Apple Arcade August 6
items.summary: Apple Arcade is surprise-adding Madden NFL 27 Arcade Edition to its service on August 6, coinciding with the nearing end of the World Cup.

items.title: SGDQ 2026 Raises $2.4M for Doctors Without Borders
items.summary: The Summer Game Dev Quick 2026 charity event successfully raised $2.4 million for Doctors Without Borders through gaming and development showcases.

items.title: Rise of the Ronin Now Available for PlayStation Plus Extra and Premium
items.summary: The open-world action RPG Rise of the Ronin, developed by veterans of The Witcher 3, is now available for PlayStation Plus Extra and Premium subscribers.

items.title: Castlevania: Belmont's Curse Confirmed for Nintendo Switch October 15
items.summary: Castlevania: Belmont's Curse will launch on Nintendo Switch on October 15, 2026, simultaneously with other platforms, eliminating previous delay concerns.

items.title: Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2 Trailer Reveals Marauder Skill Set
items.summary: A new trailer for Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2 showcases the Marauder character's devastating skill set ahead of the game's release.

items.title: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu Released with Free DLC Mission
items.summary: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu is now out, featuring a free DLC mission included with the base game.

items.title: Esports World Cup 2026: Mouz, Xtreme Gaming, and Virtus.pro Eliminated
items.summary: Mouz, Xtreme Gaming, and Virtus.pro were eliminated from the controversy-ridden Survival Stage of the Esports World Cup 2026 due to an unexpected integrity issue that disrupted matches.

items.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved Goes Gold for July 23 Early Access
items.summary: Halo: Campaign Evolved has gone gold, with Early Access kicking off on July 23, allowing fans to re-experience Master Chief's first adventure.

items.title: Dead Space Maker Glen Schofield Retires After 35 Years
items.summary: Glen Schofield, the creator of Dead Space, has announced his retirement from the video game industry after 35 years, stepping away from day-to-day operations.

items.title: Ubisoft Admits 2026 Will Be a Quiet Year for Major Launches
items.summary: In its latest financial report, Ubisoft admitted that 2026 will be relatively quiet for the company regarding major game launches.

items.title: Console Sales Expected to Plummet Nearly 20% in 2026
items.summary: A new report predicts that global console sales will drop by nearly 20% in 2026, signaling a significant downturn in the hardware market.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Uber Nears €12.5 Billion Deal to Acquire Delivery Hero
items.summary: Uber is set to acquire German food delivery company Delivery Hero in a deal valuing the company at approximately €12.5 billion ($14.34 billion), according to the Financial Times.

items.title: Amazon Leo Launches Satellite Internet Service in South Africa
items.summary: Amazon's low-earth orbit satellite venture, Amazon Leo, signed an agreement with South Africa's Herotel to launch broadband service aimed at connecting underserved rural communities.

items.title: SpaceX Shares Drop Below IPO Price for First Time
items.summary: SpaceX stock fell below its initial public offering price of $135 for the first time, just over a month after the company completed the biggest IPO ever.

items.title: TSMC Q2 Profit Expected to Hit Record on Chip Demand
items.summary: TSMC, the world's largest advanced chip manufacturer, is expected to report a 59% surge in net profit for April-June, driven by global demand for infrastructure chips.

items.title: Apple Intelligence Approved for Use on iPhones in China
items.summary: China's cyberspace regulator registered Apple's on-device generative AI service 'Apple Intelligence' for use on iPhones, marking a major expansion into the market via a partnership with Alibaba.

items.title: Canva Code 2.0 with Vibe Coding Support Now Available
items.summary: Canva has released Code 2.0, a new version of its website-building tool featuring 'Vibe Coding' support, now available to all users.

items.title: Xiaomi Rolls Out Stable Android 17-Based HyperOS 3 Updates
items.summary: Xiaomi has begun distributing stable HyperOS 3 updates based on Android 17 to its Xiaomi 17 series, including the June 2026 security patch.

items.title: Third-Party App Stores Coming to Android on July 22
items.summary: Google is set to allow third-party app stores on Android devices starting July 22, a significant shift in the platform's distribution model.

items.title: Nokia and NVIDIA Unveil AI Platform to Double Network Capacity
items.summary: Nokia and NVIDIA introduced new AI-driven network technology capable of doubling data transmission capacity by 2028 as part of their strategic partnership.

items.title: OpenAI Releases $230 Keyboard for Codex Amid Legal Battle
items.summary: OpenAI launched a light-up keyboard designed to pair with its agentic coding app Codex, releasing the hardware while facing a trade secret lawsuit from Apple.
